mutation: composite: label-assignment (seating bijection) + laplacian-pseudoinverse-flow (grounded-Laplacian electrical-flow objective) + slow-eigenvector-orthogonality (bottleneck-cut trap requires avoiding alignment with the graph's slow/Fiedler modes)
theme: seating quarrelsome metronomes around a coupled table so they entrain
title: Quarrelsome Metronomes on a Coupled Table
strategies: trivial: do-nothing identity seating (also the checker's own baseline construction)

greedy: hand extreme-magnitude metronomes to the lowest-degree seats and near-zero ones to hub seats (a 'protect the seats with fewer arms' heuristic) -- uses only each seat's own arm-count, never reasons about which side of a structural bottleneck a seat sits on, so it still leaves one side of a cut net-heavy

strong: approximate the graph's Fiedler (slow) eigenvector via shifted power iteration, order seats along it, and interleave the sorted frequency multiset from both extremes (min,max,2nd-min,2nd-max,...) along that order so every sweep-cut's net current stays near zero, then run a bounded exact local-refinement pass (resolve the true balance equations, find the worst-loaded arm, swap in whichever seat's metronome erases its imbalance, verified against the true objective) -- exploits the spectral orthogonality insight plus an exchange-argument polish
invalid: prints a degenerate constant sequence that is not a permutation -> checker rejects (Ratio 0.0)
